summary: Check-in Notification Email Evidence Details - All 11 tests passed. Verified enriched evidence_details in notification emails: Physical (GPS coords, Google Maps link, address, distance, method label GPS/QR), Video (video_display_name, connection time, platform Zoom/Teams/Meet).

backend: Test 2: Physical email with source=gps contains coordinates, Google Maps link, address, distance, method 'GPS'
Test 3: Video email contains 'Details de connexion' section with video_display_name, connection time, platform
Test 4: POST /api/checkin/manual with GPS coords creates evidence with coordinates
Test 5: POST /api/proof/{id}/checkin passes video_display_name in evidence_details
Test 6: Idempotence - second check-in does NOT send notification (atomic flag works)
Test 7: QR source produces 'QR Code' label in email
Test 8: Google Maps link format is correct: https://www.google.com/maps?q={lat},{lon}
Test 9: Video email with Microsoft Teams platform shows 'Microsoft Teams'
Test 10: Video email with Google Meet platform shows 'Google Meet'
Test 11: Integration - notify_checkin passes evidence_details to send_checkin_notification_email

seed_data_creation: Created temporary test data (appointments, participants) for unit tests - cleaned up after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Evidence details feature fully verified. Key implementation: 1) send_checkin_notification_email in email_service.py (lines 586-747) builds evidence_section based on appointment_type. 2) Physical: shows source label (GPS/QR Code/Check-in manuel), arrival time, address, coordinates with Google Maps link, distance. 3) Video: shows 'Details de connexion' section with video_display_name, connection time, platform (Zoom/Microsoft Teams/Google Meet). 4) checkin_routes.py (manual, qr/verify, gps) and proof_routes.py (checkin) all pass evidence_details to notify_checkin. 5) Idempotence via atomic flag checkin_notification_sent on participant doc.
